heading: Additional training or duty without pay: Reserves; members of the National Guard; members of the Space Force

A, title VI, § 672(c)113 Stat. 674Oct. 5, 1999“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall be effective as of , as if included in section 1039 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1996 (; [amending , Government Organization and Employees]).”February 10, 1996Public Law 104–106110 Stat. 432section 6323 of Title 5

children.label: a
children.text: A member of a reserve component of a uniformed service, or of the Space Force may, with the consent of the member, be given additional training or other duty as provided by law, without pay, as may be authorized by the Secretary concerned.

A member who performs training or other duty without pay under subsection (a) may, in the discretion of the Secretary concerned, be authorized the travel and transportation allowances prescribed by for travel performed to and from that training or duty, and, during the performance of that training or duty, be furnished with subsistence and quarters in kind or commutation thereof at a rate to be fixed by the Secretary concerned.section 452 of this title

If a military technician (dual status), as described in , is performing active duty without pay while on leave from technician employment, as authorized by , the Secretary concerned may authorize the payment of a per diem allowance to the military technician in lieu of commutation for subsistence and quarters under paragraph (1).section 10216 of title 10section 6323(d) of title 5

children.label: c
children.text: This section does not authorize compensation for work or study performed by a member of a reserve component or the Space Force in connection with correspondence courses of an armed force.

children.label: d
children.text: This section does not apply to a member who is entitled to basic pay under chapter 3 of this title.
